title: Login redirect loop after SSO change
body:
We recently switched our company's SSO provider from Okta to Microsoft Entra ID. Since the switch, some users are getting stuck in an infinite redirect loop when trying to log into TaskFlow. They click login, get redirected to Microsoft, authenticate successfully, get redirected back to TaskFlow, and then immediately get sent back to Microsoft again. Not all users are affected — maybe 30% of our team. The rest can log in fine.

We've already verified that the Entra ID app registration is correct and matches our redirect URIs. The affected users can log into other apps that use the same SSO just fine.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for the detailed write-up — the fact that only ~30% of users are affected is a really useful clue.

Can you tell me more about what the affected users have in common versus the ones who can log in fine? For example, are they in a particular group or role, did they join the company at a different time, are they using different browsers or devices, or were their accounts set up differently in the old Okta system? Even a hunch or pattern you've noticed would be helpful — sometimes the distinguishing factor isn't obvious at first.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body:
Good question. I've been trying to figure out a pattern myself. One thing I noticed is that a few of the affected users have those plus-sign email addresses — like jane+taskflow@company.com — we set some people up that way for filtering purposes. But not all affected users have that, so I'm not sure if it's related.

The other thing I noticed is that some of the affected users had their email addresses changed at some point. Like, a couple people got married and changed their last name, so their email changed in our directory, but their old email might still be associated with their TaskFlow account from when we were on Okta. I'm not 100% sure that's the connection though.

Browser-wise it seems to happen across Chrome, Edge, and Firefox so I don't think it's a browser thing. Oh, one weird thing — a couple people said they were able to get in using an incognito window, but it doesn't always work.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
That's really helpful — both of those observations feel like they could be pieces of the same puzzle. The email change angle especially stands out.

Can you tell me more about how TaskFlow identifies users when they come back from SSO? Specifically, when the redirect comes back from Entra ID, does TaskFlow match users by their email address, or by some other identifier like a unique user ID or subject claim? And related to that — for the users who changed their last name, is the email stored in their TaskFlow profile their *old* email, while Entra ID is now sending back their *new* one?

I'm wondering if what's happening is a mismatch: Entra ID authenticates them successfully and sends back their current email, but TaskFlow can't find a matching account, so it kicks them back to login. If you're able to check what email is stored in TaskFlow for one of the affected users versus what Entra ID is sending, that could confirm or rule this out.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body:
Honestly, I'm not super familiar with the technical details of how TaskFlow matches users on the backend — I'm more of an IT admin than a developer. But your theory sounds plausible to me. I did check one of the affected users who changed their name, and yeah, their TaskFlow profile still shows their old email (maiden name), while Entra ID is sending their new married-name email. So there's definitely a mismatch there.

For the plus-address users, I'm less sure what's going on. I would have assumed Entra ID sends back the same plus address, but maybe it doesn't? I haven't dug into the actual SAML claims or anything like that.

The thing that confuses me though is — if it was just an account-not-found issue, wouldn't they get an error message instead of a redirect loop? It's like TaskFlow keeps trying over and over instead of just saying 'user not found.' That's what makes me think there might be something else going on too, but I could be wrong.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"SSO redirect loop after Okta-to-Entra-ID migration due to email mismatch in user lookup","problem":"After migrating SSO from Okta to Microsoft Entra ID, approximately 30% of users experience an infinite redirect loop: they authenticate successfully with Entra ID, get redirected back to TaskFlow, but TaskFlow immediately redirects them back to Entra ID instead of establishing a session. The remaining 70% of users can log in normally.","root_cause_hypothesis":"TaskFlow's SSO callback matches returning users by email address. For affected users, the email in the Entra ID SAML/OIDC claims does not match the email stored in TaskFlow's user table. Two confirmed/suspected causes: (1) Users whose names changed (e.g., marriage) have their old email in TaskFlow but Entra ID sends their new email. (2) Users with plus-sign addresses (e.g., jane+taskflow@company.com) may be affected if Entra ID normalizes or strips the plus-tag before sending the claim. When the callback handler fails to find a matching user, it redirects to /login (or equivalent) rather than rendering an error, which triggers SSO again, creating the infinite loop. The occasional success in incognito windows may be explained by cached session cookies or stale auth state in normal browsing contributing to the loop.","reproduction_steps":["Identify a user whose TaskFlow profile email differs from their current Entra ID email (e.g., a user who changed their last name)","Have that user attempt to log into TaskFlow via SSO","Observe: Entra ID authenticates successfully and redirects back to TaskFlow","TaskFlow fails to match the user and redirects back to the login/SSO flow","The cycle repeats indefinitely"],"environment":"TaskFlow with SSO authentication, recently migrated from Okta to Microsoft Entra ID. Affects ~30% of users across all browsers (Chrome, Edge, Firefox). Entra ID app registration and redirect URIs verified as correct.","severity":"high","impact":"Approximately 30% of the team is completely locked out of TaskFlow with no workaround (incognito is unreliable). This is a blocking issue for those users.","recommended_fix":"1. **Immediate:** In the SSO callback handler, find where user lookup fails and add a proper error response (e.g., 'No matching account found') instead of redirecting to /login. This breaks the loop and makes the problem visible. 2. **Root cause:** Change user matching to use a stable, immutable identifier (such as the OIDC `sub` claim or Entra ID object ID) rather than email address. This is the long-term fix. 3. **Short-term data fix:** For currently affected users, update their TaskFlow profile emails to match their current Entra ID email, or backfill the Entra ID `sub`/object ID into their user record. 4. **Investigate plus-sign handling:** Check what Entra ID actually sends in the email claim for plus-sign addresses and whether TaskFlow's lookup does an exact match or normalizes.","proposed_test_case":"Create a test user in TaskFlow with email old@company.com. Configure the SSO IdP to return new@company.com for that user. Attempt login and verify that (a) the user is not stuck in a redirect loop and (b) the system either matches the user by stable ID or displays a clear error message about the email mismatch.","information_gaps":["Exact identifier used in TaskFlow's SSO callback for user matching (email vs. sub claim vs. other) — needs code inspection","Whether Entra ID normalizes or strips plus-sign email addresses in its claims — needs SAML/OIDC response inspection","Whether the incognito success pattern is related to stale session cookies or a timing/race condition","Whether any affected users fall outside both the name-change and plus-sign categories"]}
